skill.name: explain-expert
skill.description: Analyze codebases and produce structured technical overviews of components, dependencies, interactions, deployment architecture, and runtime behavior.
skill.summary: Create structured architecture explanations for unfamiliar codebases.
skill.author: nexteacc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns, but manual review found only prompt text and marketplace metadata. The cited lines describe repository analysis, architecture explanation, and example documentation workflows, with no executable cryptography, shell commands, network calls, file writes, prompt injection, or secret handling. The static findings are false positives, and the prompt-only skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at these locations. Manual review found descriptive words inside skill instructions and example marketplace content, not cryptographic code or security-sensitive hash usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language descriptions such as analysis framework, components, and runtime behavior. No code, cryptographic API, hashing function, or credential-related logic is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance patterns where the skill asks the model to scan repository structure and provide a technical overview. This is the stated purpose of the skill and does not include stealth, persistence, exfiltration, or host enumeration instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reconnaissance-like wording is limited to benign codebase review and documentation. There is no evidence of commands, network access, hidden collection, or attacker-oriented behavior.

content.value_statement: Unfamiliar repositories are hard to understand without a repeatable review process. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured analysis of components, dependencies, deployment, and runtime behavior.
actual_capabilities: Scans repository structure to identify directories, configuration files, and entry points.
Reviews key files such as package manifests, Docker files, READMEs, and main source files.
Maps external libraries, frameworks, and major dependency roles.
Traces startup paths, request handling, workflows, errors, and background tasks.
Explains component responsibilities, interactions, data flow, APIs, and service patterns.
Summarizes deployment setup, build steps, environments, infrastructure, and containers.
limitations: It depends on files that are available in the current workspace.
It may miss behavior that only appears during live execution.
It does not modify code or create implementation changes by default.
Large repositories may require focused scope to keep the explanation concise.

use_cases.title: Accelerate project onboarding
use_cases.description: Understand the repository layout, entry points, core modules, and request flow before making changes.

use_cases.title: Document existing architecture
use_cases.description: Create a clear technical overview for planning, handoff, audits, or team alignment.

use_cases.title: Prepare system documentation
use_cases.description: Turn source files and configuration into a structured explanation of system behavior and deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Codebase Overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an unfamiliar repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this codebase at a high level. Identify the main directories, entry points, components, and how they fit together.

prompt_templates.title: Component Interaction Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the core components in this repository. Explain each responsibility, the main interfaces, and the data or control flow between them.

prompt_templates.title: Deployment And Runtime Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the deployment architecture and runtime behavior. Cover build steps, environments, containers, startup flow, request handling, and background work.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Architecture Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation for handoff or planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete technical architecture brief for this codebase. Include repository structure, dependencies, components, interactions, deployment setup, runtime behavior, and notable assumptions.

output_examples.input: Explain this codebase at a high level.
output: The project is organized around a service entry point, shared configuration, domain modules, and tests.
The main application initializes configuration, registers services, and routes incoming requests to feature modules.
The deployment path uses a build step, environment configuration, and container packaging.

output_examples.input: Map the core components and their interactions.
output: The API layer receives requests and validates inputs before calling domain services.
Domain services coordinate business workflows and use repository classes for persistence.
Shared utilities handle logging, configuration, error formatting, and external integration boundaries.
The application starts by loading environment settings, creating shared clients, and registering handlers.
Runtime behavior centers on request processing, background jobs, and structured error handling.
Deployment requires build artifacts, runtime environment variables, and infrastructure dependencies.

best_practices: Start with repository structure and configuration before reading individual modules.
Trace one representative execution flow from entry point to output.
Separate confirmed facts from assumptions when documentation is incomplete.
anti_patterns: Do not rewrite or refactor code when the user only asked for explanation.
Do not skip deployment files when explaining runtime behavior.
Do not present guesses as facts when key files are missing.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes repository structure, key files, dependencies, components, interactions, deployment setup, and runtime behavior.

faq.question: Does it change my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for explanation and documentation, not code modification.

faq.question: Can it work with any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. The framework is language-neutral, but the quality depends on available files and project clarity.

faq.question: Does it run the application?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on static review unless the user separately asks for execution or testing.

faq.question: What output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect structured prose covering architecture, components, dependencies, interactions, deployment, and runtime flow.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when onboarding to a project, preparing documentation, reviewing architecture, or explaining how a system works.
